topic: Indian Coasts, Islands, Straits and Maritime Geography
subtopic: Coastal-plain contrasts, island origin, channels, ports, ecosystems and strategic location

content.issueStory: India’s maritime geography is a system of two contrasting coasts, island chains of different origin, narrow channels, monsoon sea routes, ports and highly productive but exposed ecosystems.
content.coreConcept: The western and eastern coastal plains differ in width, submergence-emergence character, river mouths and delta development. Lakshadweep is predominantly coral, while Andaman-Nicobar is a tectonic island arc close to crucial Southeast Asian sea lanes.
dimensions: Western coast is generally narrower, more indented and associated with estuaries and natural harbour settings.
Eastern coast is generally broader and receives large delta-building rivers.
Lakshadweep islands are coral atolls and reefs on submarine ridge foundations.
Andaman-Nicobar islands form an arcuate tectonic chain with volcanic and seismic activity.
Channels such as Ten Degree, Eight Degree and Six Degree organise island and regional mapping.
Mangroves, reefs, ports, fisheries, tourism and naval access create ecological-strategic overlap.
content.constitutionalLink: Coastal Regulation Zone rules, Island Coastal Regulation, marine protected areas, disaster plans and UNCLOS-based rights govern a sensitive maritime domain.
content.dataReport: Primary uploaded sources: STATIC_03 Indian Geography coasts, islands, ports and waterways sections plus PYQ map associations. This note uses stable conceptual relationships and PYQ patterns; update event-specific data from current official sources before using it in a live mains answer.
content.caseStudy: Great Nicobar sits close to the Malacca-bound shipping corridor, creating strategic opportunity alongside exceptional ecological and indigenous-community sensitivity.
argumentsFor: Islands extend maritime reach and monitoring.
Ports and coastal corridors support trade.
Reefs and mangroves protect biodiversity and shorelines.
argumentsAgainst: Small islands have limited freshwater and carrying capacity.
Coastal infrastructure can disrupt sediment and habitats.
Cyclones, tsunami and sea-level rise create compound exposure.
content.pyqLink: PYQs test island origin, channel-location pairs, east-west coast contrasts, ports and mangroves. Map every named feature relative to nearby land and sea.

answerFramework.intro: Indian Coasts, Islands, Straits and Maritime Geography is best understood as a spatial process in which origin, controlling conditions, resulting pattern and human significance must be connected.
body: Compare east and west coastal plains.
Separate coral Lakshadweep from tectonic Andaman-Nicobar origins.
Map channels, ports and adjacent international sea lanes.
Balance maritime strategy with island carrying capacity and ecosystem safeguards.
answerFramework.conclusion: A high-scoring geography answer converts process into pattern, locates the pattern with examples, and closes with risk-sensitive or resource-sensitive planning.

mainsExamples: Gulf of Kachchh as an indented western coastal setting.
Barren Island in the Andaman Sea as an active volcano.
prelimsFacts: Lakshadweep is mainly coral in origin.
Ten Degree Channel separates Andaman and Nicobar groups.
Eight Degree Channel lies between Minicoy and Maldives.
Six Degree Channel lies south of Great Nicobar toward Indonesia.
Large east-flowing rivers form major deltas.
Mangroves attenuate waves but do not eliminate cyclone risk.

revisionBox.answer: Do not describe Lakshadweep as a volcanic island arc.

revisionBox.answer: Gulf of Kachchh as an indented western coastal setting.

revisionBox.answer: Balance maritime strategy with island carrying capacity and ecosystem safeguards.
